title: Pipeline Stage Order
description:
## What Runs When?

A team is designing a CI/CD pipeline for a containerized web app. Which ordering of stages reflects the **typical, sensible** flow from a fresh commit to a running deployment?

options.text: Build the code → run the tests → package the artifact/image → deploy
Deploy to production → run the tests → build the code → package the artifact
Package the artifact → deploy → build the code → run the tests
Run the tests → deploy → build the code → package the artifact

explanation: A pipeline moves from source to a deployed app in a logical order: **build** (check out and compile the code), **test** (unit/integration tests and static analysis — you must have built before you can test), **package** (produce a versioned artifact or container image from code that already passed), and finally **deploy** that validated artifact. Every other option deploys before testing or builds after packaging, which would ship untested or non-existent code — exactly what a pipeline exists to prevent.

hints.title: Think about dependencies
hints.body: You cannot test code you haven't built, and you should never deploy code you haven't tested. Each stage produces what the next one needs.
